subject: ali-yahya

sectors_excited.name: AI x crypto / decentralized AI
sectors_excited.why: The most important frontier I see. As AI agents proliferate, they need native economic rails — machine-to-machine payments, verifiable compute and inference, and onchain identity. Crypto is the only credibly neutral substrate for agents to transact and for proving provenance of data and models. Proof-of-personhood becomes essential to distinguish humans from agents online. This is precisely where my ML background and my crypto conviction converge.

sectors_excited.name: Stablecoins & onchain payments
sectors_excited.why: The clearest product-market fit in all of crypto. With US regulatory clarity now in place, stablecoins are moving real volume — remittances, B2B settlement, dollar savings in inflationary economies. Dollars on open, programmable, global rails. This is the wedge that onboards the next hundreds of millions of people.

sectors_excited.name: DePIN (decentralized physical infrastructure)
sectors_excited.why: Token incentives are the right tool to solve the cold-start problem that killed prior infrastructure projects — bootstrapping real-world networks for compute/GPUs, wireless, energy, and storage. Decentralized compute markets also feed the AI thesis directly, which is why I find this category structurally compelling rather than narrative-driven.

sectors_excited.name: Decentralized social & onchain identity
sectors_excited.why: Protocols that give users ownership of their social graph and data, with composable applications built on top. Combined with onchain identity and proof-of-personhood, this is how we rebuild the social layer of the internet with incentives aligned to users instead of platforms.

sectors_concerned.name: Memecoins / pure speculation
sectors_concerned.why: The casino dynamics are extractive and crowd out builders. They generate real revenue and attention, but they train a generation to treat crypto as a slot machine — the opposite of why this technology matters. Bad for legitimacy and a serious misallocation of talent and capital.

sectors_concerned.name: Decentralization theater / re-centralization
sectors_concerned.why: Systems that market themselves as decentralized while concentrating control in a foundation, a single sequencer, or a multisig. The entire point of this technology is credible neutrality and verifiability; shortcuts there are a long-term liability even when they're expedient in the short term.

risk_regime.why: I think in decades, not cycles. My conviction is in the multi-year build-out — AI x crypto, stablecoins, DePIN — and the fundamentals there are stronger than ever with real usage and regulatory clarity. Near-term prices run hot and speculation is frothy, so this is not a short-term trading call; structurally I'm a long-term optimist, and we keep funding builders through every part of the cycle.

notes: All items are marked extrapolated: they are inferences from my consistent, thesis-driven public stance rather than restatements of a specific dated quote, so I've attached no citations. My conviction sits deliberately at the sector and infrastructure level — I don't make individual token calls publicly — so the token arrays are intentionally empty rather than manufactured to fill a bucket. Time-boxed to what I know as of 2026-03-31.
